entry.title: Unlocking the Power of Version Management in SAP Analytics Cloud for Planning
entry.content:
Version Management within SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) for Planning is an area that needs to be organized and pristine.  This blog explores the differences between Private and Public versions and how to manage versions effectively using examples from best-practice business content, such as the Rapid FP&A Suite for SAP S/4HANA.

Understanding Private and Public Versions in SAP Analytics Cloud

Private Versions and Public Versions serve distinct purposes in SAP Analytics Cloud:

  • Private Versions are individual workspaces where planners can experiment with scenarios and updates without affecting others. Private Versions allow users to test different planning approaches in isolation.

  • Public Versions are shared, organization-wide Versions that serve as the official source of truth for FP&A data. When a planner inputs data into a Public Version, their inputs are captured in a Private Version. The planner must then actively choose to "publish" their changes so that they appear in the Public Version.

Establish a Single Planning Version

The process within the Rapid FP&A Suite uses a single, write-enabled, public PLAN version—the Working Plan—for all active planning activities. This approach minimizes confusion by avoiding multiple editable Plan Versions simultaneously. Multiple editable Versions (e.g., Plan_1, Plan_2) upfront, can:

  • Confuse planners about where to submit their data.

  • Require constant updates to input forms and associated security settings for each Version.

  • Introduce excessive variability into workflows, leading to errors.

To simplify the process, the SAP consultants behind Rapid FP&A suggest:

  • Focus on a Single PLAN Version: Using a single PLAN Version ensures planners always know where to input their data. This reduces confusion and eliminates the administrative burden of maintaining multiple editable Versions.

  • Pre-Configured Input Forms in Rapid FP&A: Input forms in the Rapid FP&A Suite are pre-configured to filter on the PLAN Version, ensuring planners work in the "Working Plan" Version.

  • Leverage Data Actions: The Rapid FP&A Suite includes pre-configured Data Actions that allow you to copy any PLAN data into finalized, version-specific names like Target2025 or Budget2024. Once copied, these versions become read-only and are naturally referred to by their specific names (e.g., Forecast2025) for clarity and consistency.

Why does this approach work? It focuses planners on completing one version of the Plan before moving to another, avoiding the complexity and errors of managing multiple editable versions simultaneously.

Managing Data Within Private Versions

Private Versions offer a flexible environment to refine and validate planning scenarios before publishing to Public Versions. Here are some best practices:

How to Use Private Versions Effectively

Planners must flow their plan inputs through Private Versions, regardless of how Public Versions are managed, so educating Planners on how to use Private Versions optimally is crucial. Proper use of Private Versions ensures accurate updates to the plan and provides  flexibility:

  • Experiment and Refine: Use Private Versions to model scenarios, refine your inputs, and validate changes without impacting others.

  • Publish with Purpose: Once all updates are finalized, publish the changes to the Public Version to make them available for organizational use. Under the Rapid FP&A Suite, published updates flow into the PLAN Version (Working Plan), which admins can later archive into specific Version names (e.g., Budget2026) once all plans are collected.

  • Utilize Tools for Confidence: Features like the “Revert” function and “Version History Panel” provide a safety net to undo mistakes during refinement of your Private version. Remember: Reverting is unavailable after publishing it in the Public Version.

Tip: The Rapid FP&A Suite includes pre-configured input forms and sample data, allowing you to practice updating and publishing Private Versions in a "practice" environment.

Best Practices for Managing Public Versions

Creating multiple specific Version names (e.g., Budget2026, Forecast2025) at the start of every planning cycle can be problematic. This approach requires updating all input forms and reports to align with each new Version name, creating inefficiencies and potential confusion. Additionally, frequent changes to Version names can leave users unsure where to input their plan data, causing unnecessary delays and errors.

Use Categories to Organize Your Versions

You can use the category property to organize your versions by type. Categories help distinguish versions and streamline management. The following categories can help organize versions:

  • Actual, Budget, Plan, Forecast, Rolling Forecast

A model can have one public Actuals version, typically updated via import from a data source like S/4HANA. However, private copies of this version can be created for analysis or testing. Categories like Forecast allow you to create multiple forecast versions to explore optimistic, baseline, or worst-case outcomes.

Under Best Practices in the Rapid FP&A Suite, planners will focus on one scenario at a time (e.g., baseline) within the PLAN Version. Once the scenario is finalized, it can be archived under a version-specific name such as Baseline2025 or Optimistic2025, and then the planner can move on to the following scenario. This approach ensures planners complete one scenario before moving to another, avoiding confusion and streamlining the process.

Hold Actual Data in a Read-Only Version

  • Store finalized Actual data in a read-only ACTUAL Version. This ensures the integrity of historical data and provides a clear baseline for variance analysis.

Archive Specific Versions for Reporting

  • Instead of constantly updating input forms and security for new write-enabled Versions, take snapshots of the working PLAN Version at key points in the planning cycle. Archive these snapshots into finalized, read-only Versions (e.g., Budget2026, Forecast2025) using pre-built Data Actions from the Rapid FP&A Suite.

  • This approach preserves the integrity of snapshot data while avoiding the repetitive task of reconfiguring templates and security for every user or group needing write access to a plan version.

How Rapid FP&A Suite Supports Version Management

The Rapid FP&A Suite for SAP S/4HANA is a valuable toolset for:

  1. Testing and Experimentation

    • Use sample data to explore how updates to Private Versions flow into Public Versions.

  2. Streamlined Workflows

    • Discover how we maintain a single write-enabled PLAN Version for all planning activities.

    • See how admins can create finalized, locked-down Versions efficiently by copying a version from PLAN

  3. Reducing Administrative Overhead

    • You will see how pre-configured input forms align with the active working PLAN Version.

    • With only one active PLAN version all the time, you simplify security and configuration management

  4. Preserving Data Integrity

    • Finalized Versions like Budget2026 or Forecast2025 are clearly labeled and locked for accurate reporting.

    • Any other interim snapshots of PLAN can be archived for reviews or status meetings.

Benefits for FP&A Teams?

  • Planners: Clarity on where to input data with a single active Version.

  • Admins: Efficiency in managing Versions with reduced maintenance effort.

  • Stakeholders: Reliable, finalized Versions ready for variance analysis and decision-making.

Getting Started

Ready to unlock the full potential of Version Management? Leverage the Rapid FP&A Suite for SAP S/4HANA as a toolset to test and refine your planning processes. Explore its pre-configured content, including input forms, data actions, and sample scenarios, to simplify and standardize your workflows. The best part? You can download the suite in minutes and review these processes using sample data—no integration is required during your discovery phase.

How do you Download the free Rapid FP&A Suite?
Downloading the Rapid FP&A Suite is straightforward and hassle-free.
Follow these steps to get started:
1. Log in to your SAP Analytics Cloud account.   Please don't use a production tenant.
2. Navigate to the "Content Network" on the left-hand side panel of SAC
3. Select "Business Content"
4. Search for "Rapid" in the upper right-hand 'Search' box - you should see Rapid Finance, Workforce, Sales
4. Follow the instructions to complete the download and installation of each of the Rapid modules (Rapid Finance, Rapid Sales, Rapid Workforce).

Let’s make planning Rapid, Simple, and Standard with SAP Analytics Cloud!

entry.title: How can SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite support your journey to SAP S/4HANA Cloud?
entry.content:
Yesterday, while presenting SAP’s financial results for FY2024, our CEO Christian Klein explained that we exceeded our cloud goals, accelerating cloud revenue and current cloud backlog growth against a much larger base. He also mentioned that half of our cloud order entry are now including AI. Clearly, our aim is to provide our customers with a suite of cloud-based applications that provides the most valuable data in business and powers unique AI capabilities. In this blog post, I would like to present how SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite – both running on SAP Business Technology Platform – are at the heart of SAP’s strategy and support our customers in their journey from legacy ERPs to SAP S/4HANA Cloud.


We know that for many SAP customers, the transition to SAP S/4HANA can be challenging, because someone in the organization has added a lot of custom code to your ERP 😉. That’s why SAP has created RISE with SAP, a packaged offering that helps you transform on-premise SAP ERP systems, modernize business processes, and become more agile in the cloud. And in RISE with SAP, you will find anything you need to fulfill your needs.

Let me be a bit more specific: every company in the world uses software to run their operations. We all know the most efficient and effective way is to use standard software like our SAP S/4HANA ERP. At the same time, we are fully aware that every organization in the world is unique and gains competitive advantage through their very specific processes. This might be the way they run processes for their customers because they offer unique experiences, or the way they engage with their suppliers because they may be faster in the supply chain processes, or it might be their HR processes which allows them to retain the best talent in the market… whatever it is, that makes your company unique, it should be reflected in your software because no one likes manual processes in 2025. In the context of migrating from SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA Cloud, following the Clean Core strategy is a powerful opportunity: by eliminating custom code, technical debt, and standardizing the processes and data that underpin ERP operations, your business will benefit from more best practices while being able to customize what it needs to differentiate itself. And when it comes to standardization to achieve clean core with proper extensions and integrations, SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite on SAP BTP enter the game!

SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite add great value to your Cloud ERP

SAP Build allows you to extend and personalize SAP S/4HANA to meet your needs. It accelerates development and automation of extensions with low-code, pro-code, and generative AI tools. It enables stable, secure, and scalable ERP extensions that adhere to a prescribed extensibility model, with compliance to code quality standards and best practices. Tailored for every SAP customer, any size, any landscape, any industry, SAP Build is the best of suite tools for developers and key users collaborating in AI-powered ERP extension development.

Let me say it simply: SAP Build adds great value to your Cloud ERP. You will get more out of your cloud ERP investment with SAP Build. Just because it lets you easily customize, extend, integrate and automate business processes in your SAP S/4HANA Cloud solution to meet specific requirements while keeping the core clean. Watch our webinar “Extend SAP S/4HANA Cloud with SAP Build: your Free Toolkit”: in an hour, you will discover how you can leverage SAP Build’s low-code, pro-code, and generative AI development tools to drive innovation, automate processes, and supercharge your S/4HANA Cloud experience.

SAP Integration Suite is an iPaaS that helps you quickly integrate on-premises and cloud-based processes, services, applications, events, and data. For sure, it also adds value to your ERP: it connects business processes with proven pre-built integrations, APIs, connectors, and best practices. It provides upgrade-stable interfaces, proper monitoring and error resolution capabilities, and actively used and well-documented connectors.

For What Use Cases?

Let me give you very concrete examples where SAP Build is very helpful to address specific challenges in your Lead to Cash processes:

  • Digitize in-store cash sales processing
  • Automate the creation of sales orders from Excel files
  • Automate mass maintenance of billing blocks
  • Automate the approvals of return requests
  • Automate the creation of sales orders from unstructured data in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • Automate managing credit blocks on sales orders

and more…

I can also share many use cases that could improve your Source to Pay processes:

  • Centralize and streamline supplier communications with dedicated business sites
  • Mass maintenance of purchase contracts
  • Automate the creation of purchase requisitions from structured data in SAP S/4HANA
  • Automate the processing of paper-based invoices
  • Automatically populate missing material codes in purchase requisitions
  • Track sustainability data across your supply chain

and more…

You will find complementary use cases on our SAP Build web page.

And there is more: on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, you can browse 500+ pre-built content packages for SAP Build and 600+ pre-built content packages for SAP Integration Suite. If you have developed custom code in your on-premises ERP in the past to address such use cases or specific needs, you can explore this vast catalog to see if some of these packages might be a good fit for your cloud ERP migration process. Indeed it is very important to know that SAP Build comes with pre-built content for ECC: you can use these SAP Build packages on ECC already to standardize some extensions, and then all the extensions developed on ECC with SAP Build will taken to S/4HANA during the migration.

Insights from Customers

What I presented above is not only theoretical: customers who have been through this SAP S/4HANA journey share valuable insights that can really help you. I have selected two few interesting stories, in different industries.

Sony Pictures

Let me start with Sony Pictures Networks: relying on the legacy system, SAP ECC, their highly customized and siloed infrastructure struggled to align with the company's integrated business processes. And decentralized data presented SAP challenges in consolidating business reports until the end.

Sony decided to remaster their operations for the future through next-generation ERP, with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, and Enterprise Automation, combining SAP Signavio, SAP Build And SAP Integration Suite. Indeed, SAP Signavio radically accelerates business process transformation, and I engage you to read this blog post explaining how customers on SAP ECC systems can prepare for an SAP S/4HANA transformation with SAP Signavio.

“By migrating to SAP S/4HANA and adopting SAP Business Technology Platform, we can keep the core clean. This strategy allows for faster development of any new technologies or any new process rollouts and enables us to take advantage of generative AI as part of our way forward” explains Mahesh Patil, SVP and Head of IT – Infrastructure, Integration, and Data Lake, Sony Pictures Networks India. And the results are excellent:

  • 100% automated process mining, reducing effort from weeks to days
  • 50% of interfaces managed through SAP Integration Suite
  • ~85% faster mobile app creation, reducing effort from four weeks to three days

Hitachi

Would you like another good example? Let’s take Hitachi High-Tech Corporation: they replaced their highly customized multinational business landscape by investing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud in a two-tier public and private cloud model. With a fit-to-standard ethos, the company can now focus on supporting business innovation across its subsidiaries using a side-by-side approach on SAP Business Technology Platform.

“Our new policy is to fit our business processes to standard as much as possible, reducing our need for customizations” says Yuri Yasuda, Manager Corporate DX Department, Digital Transformation Business Group, Hitachi High-Tech Corporation, who shares impressive KPIs:

  • 94% reduction in customization footprint
  • 18x faster handling of solution updates
  • 100% of on-site maintenance requirements eliminated

You can also find inspiration in the S/4HANA projects that Mahindra Group, PX Energy, LEOCH, Farys, Delta Light or Prestige have successfully implemented.

Start your S/4HANA journey with the support of SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite!

I hope that you’re now convinced that SAP BTP in general and SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite in particular are very powerful to ease your migration to SAP S/4HANA. To start this journey, watch our short video and request a customized demo: together, let’s make your business unstoppable!

entry.title: Integrating Business Event Logging with SAP Signavio
entry.content:
Integrating Business Event Logging

This blog explores various aspects of integrating Business Event Logging (BEL) with SAP Signavio Process Intelligence, focusing on data models, integration scenarios, and the use of SAP Business Technology Platform.

BEL Data Model

In the data model of Business Event Logging, we store the following essential information in an event header for each Business Event:

  • Event type (Object type + operation)
  • Object keys
  • The user that executed the underlying action
  • Timestamp of the action/event creation
  • TCode of the transaction (Available in OP from S/4HANA OP 2023 FPS3)
  • Transaction ID which represents the technical session in which an event was created. This helps   identify the events that were created in the same session.

 Data dependent on the Business Event:

  • Field Changes (Fieldname, new and old value, as contained in the business event)
  • (Optionally) All Business Event Data of the business event. This is stored as a JSON string. You can choose this option in the configuration UI, where you activate business event logging.

We offer a data extraction view supporting full load and delta handling (via CDC):

  • Event Header - C_BusEvtLogEventDEX_2
  • Field Changes - C_BusEvtLogPayloadDEX_2
  • Complete Business EventData JSON format - C_BuEvLgEvtFullPayloadJSONDEX (OP from S/4HANA OP 2023 FPS2)

Integration and Communication Scenarios in BEL

You can connect SAP Signavio Process Intelligence with Business Event Logging (as part of SAP S/4HANA or SAP BTP, ABAP Environment) either directly or via SAP Datasphere (default option).

Depending on the product, you use different communication scenarios to extract or access business events, field changes, or full business event data.

Communication scenarios in SAP BTP, ABAP Environment (2411 or higher):

  • : For data federation or data extraction, or 
  • (Generic) SAP_COM_0532: For data replication. Use the schema API_BusEvtLogEvent with this communication scenario.

Communication scenarios in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition (CE 2411 or higher)

  • SAP_COM_0531 Or , or
  • SAP_COM_0532: For data replication from Business Event Logging to target systems.

Use the following CDS views with SAP_COM_0531 and SAP_COM_0532 scenarios:

  • Business Event Header Data (v2):  C_BusEvtLogEventDEX_2
  • Business Event Property Changes (v2): C_BusEvtLogPayloadDEX_2
  • Business Event Data: C_BuEvLgEvtFullPayloadJSONDEX

Alternatively for SAP_COM_0532 you can also use the schema API_BusEvtLogEvent.

For more information on SAP_COM_0531 see Integrating CDI
For more information on SAP_COM_0532, see Integrating CDS Views Using SAP Datasphere and Integrating SQL Services Using SAP Datasphere

Need to Know for SAP S/4HANA (On-Premise)/Cloud Private Edition

SAP Signavio can be connected to SAP S/4HANA On-Premise either directly or via SAP Datasphere.

  1. Integration via SAP Datasphere (SAP S/4HANA – SAP Datasphere – SAP Signavio)
    Using Cloud Connector SAP S/4HANA On-Premise system can be connected to SAP Datasphere. The Cloud Connector serves as a link between applications in SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP Datasphere) and on-premise or private cloud systems (SAP S/4HANA), enabling the use of existing on-premise assets without exposing the entire internal landscape.
  2. b)     Direct integration, SAP S/4HANA On-Premise – SAP Signavio

Using on-premises extractor, on-premises source system can be connected to Process Data Management in SAP Signavio.

Connect SAP Datasphere or SAP Signavio Process Intelligence directly using these views for data extraction:

  • Business Event Header Data (v2): C_BusEvtLogEventDEX_2
  • Business Event Property Changes (v2): C_BusEvtLogPayloadDEX_2
  • Business Event Data: C_BuEvLgEvtFullPayloadJSONDEX

Additional links/general blogs regarding on-premise/private cloud integration are listed below:

  • Consume CDS Views from SAP S/4 HANA on-premise into SAP Data Warehouse Cloud, leveraging the ABAP Pipeline Engine (APE)
  • How to use SAP Data Intelligence with SAP Cloud Connector
  • Preparing Cloud Connector Connectivity
  • Prepare Connectivity to SAP S/4HANA On-Premise

Usage of SAP Datasphere

To leverage the replication capabilities in SAP Datasphere, follow these steps:

  1. Create a Connection
  2. Create a New Replication Flow
  3. Select the CDS or the Schema/table In Load Type, choose Initial and Delta for delta replication or Initial Only for the initial load.
  4. Add/Rename a target table. Create and deploy tables to generate it.
    1. Use SAP HANA on Datasphere as target and use the SAP HANA Connector in Signavio to retrieve the data
    2. Use the new SAP Datasphere Replication Flow Connector to directly pass the data from source system to SAP Signavio (currently in BETA)

For more information, see Integrating CDS Views Using SAP Datasphere.

entry.title: SAP Fiori launchpad: Hiding empty spaces and pages
entry.content:
What is this feature about?

Overview

Hiding empty spaces and pages is a new feature of SAP Fiori launchpad.

Essentially, this means that empty spaces and pages are removed from the space menu and are no longer shown in the App Finder.

To be more precise, if pages or spaces are empty—meaning they don’t contain any (1) role-based content or (2) device-type-based content—then it makes no sense to expose them. In such cases, it would serve no purpose for users to access them.

Benefits

The key benefit of this approach is that it enhances the user experience by preventing end users from encountering empty content.

Additionally, this feature gives administrators greater flexibility. They can organize content without worrying about creating empty pages or spaces since such pages will automatically be hidden from end users.

Restriction

End user personalization is not considered, mainly to optimize performance.

If a page is empty and users personalized it while the feature is disabled, enabling the feature later will cause that page to disappear, as only the original content defined by the administrators is considered.

Similarly, if users remove all tiles from a page through personalization, making it empty, the page won’t be hidden because it became empty due to user actions.

 

How to enable this feature?

As an administrator, it is possible to enable or disable the display of empty spaces and pages by toggling the parameter ‘SPACES_HIDE_EMPTY_SPACES_PAGES’ using the 'Manage Launchpad Settings' application.

  • If the toggle is ON, the space menu should hide any empty spaces and pages.
  • If the toggle is OFF, the space menu shows all spaces and pages, regardless of their content. 

 

To enable or disable this setting, users must Edit and Save it. The changes to this configuration will take effect only after a refresh.

 

Feature release outlook

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

  • This feature is available with 2502 and will be offered as opt-in in this initial release, i.e., disabled by default.
  • For further information refer to the official documentation here.

SAP BTP ABAP environment 

  • This feature is available with 2502 and will be offered as opt-in in this initial release, meaning it will be disabled by default and configurable.
  • This feature is planned* to be offered as opt-out with 2505, meaning it will be enabled by default and configurable. With 2508, it is planned* to be enabled by default and no longer configurable. (*Disclaimer: our plans are subject to change)

SAP Integrated Business Planning 

  • This feature is available with 2502 and will be offered as opt-in in this initial release, i.e., disabled by default.

entry.title: Upload of a custom logo to be displayed in an SAP Fiori launchpad shell
entry.content:
What is this feature about?

Overview

This feature enables users to replace the standard SAP logo with a custom logo by uploading an image file in the SAP Fiori launchpad.

As a result, the SAP Fiori launchpad shell can display a customized logo.

Benefits

Previously, customizing a logo required using custom themes via the UI theme designer to create these themes and replace the standard SAP logo with a customer-specific one. With this new feature, the benefit is a significant reduction in the administrative effort needed to update the logo in the SAP Fiori launchpad shell.

Restriction

Even though this feature allows customization of the logo to be displayed in the SAP Fiori launchpad shell, users are unable to revert to the original SAP logo once it’s replaced. Nevertheless, they have the option to replace the uploaded custom logo with another new image file if needed.

How to enable this feature?

As an administrator, it is possible to upload an image file as company logo using the parameter ‘COMPANY_LOGO_URL’ via the 'Manage Launchpad Settings' application as depicted in the screenshots below.

 

This parameter allows users to add their logo to all themes, including SAP standard themes and custom themes. The uploaded custom logo always takes precedence over any other logos (for e.g a logo defined by the custom theme).

It is also possible to maintain an accessible text for the custom logo using the parameter ‘COMPANY_LOGO_ACCESSIBLE_TEXT’.

This ensures that all users, including those with disabilities, can understand its purpose. This can be done by adding descriptive alt text (for e.g "CompanyName Logo, link to homepage") to aid in navigation and understanding.

To update the above two settings, users must Edit and Save it. The changes to the configurations will take effect only after a refresh.

 

Feature release outlook

This feature is included in the 2408 release for:

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud public Edition (refer to the official documentation here)
  • SAP Integrated Business Planning 
  • SAP BTP ABAP environment

entry.title: Accelerate your period-end journal entries with AI-assisted Journal Upload Cases
entry.content:
With the S/4Hana Cloud Private Edition 2023 FPS3 delivery, SAP would deliver a feature that accelerates period-end journal entries with AI aid. Period-end journal entries are necessary for a period-closing activity. Finance teams spend considerable time curating data according to policies and reporting needs. SAP Business AI for Finance offers AI-assisted Journal Upload Cases that streamline operations, automate processes, and generate period-end entries aligned with posting policies faster than ever. This empowers better business decisions and transforms your team into a lean strategic finance function.  This solution is ready to use for your specific business needs without the need for IT support. (Use of this feature may require some additional commercial agreements, please get in touch with your SAP account executive for further details.) 

This blog post explains the steps to using AI-assisted Journal Upload Cases. One example of recurring period-end tasks is creating accrual postings for shuttle bus expenses incurred by different departments. This is one example of recurring tasks that can be seamlessly performed utilizing AI-assisted Journal Upload Cases.

1. Prerequisites

AI-Assisted Journal Upload Cases require AI to be scoped in an S/4Hana Cloud Private Edition system. SAP has delivered an ISLM (Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management) scenario, FINS_ACDOC_AI_MJUC, which needs to be deployed and activated in a customer system.

Before activating the above scenario, you need to get AI service keys. Please follow the required SAP ticket process to get the necessary keys by creating a ticket on component CA-S4H-BTPAI. Once you have the required data (OAuth 2.0 Client Profile & Service Key (JSON format)) available, you need to update the same in the system using the below steps.

Update the service key details in the ISLM scenario via the below IMG tree:

ABAP Platform > Application Server > Basis Services > Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management > Service Connections for Machine Learning Infrastructure > Maintain Intelligent Scenario Connections

After updating the AI key information, the ISLM scenario must be deployed and activated in the system. This can be done using the Fiori app (App ID F4470): Intelligent Scenario Management.

 

 

1.1 Authorizations

The Fiori apps for posting policy and journal cases are added to the below artifacts:

Business Catalog: SAP_SFIN_BC_GL_JE_PROC (General Ledger—Journal Entry Processing)

Technical Catalog: SAP_TC_FIN_ACC_COMMON (SAP Financials - Accounting: Fiori Apps)

Business Group: SAP_SFIN_BCG_DOC_ENTRY (Journal Entries)

Business Role: SAP_BR_GL_ACCOUNTANT (General Ledger Accountant)

1.2 Number Ranges

Number range object: IJU_CASE

Use T-Code SNRO and maintain a number range interval for the journal case object.

2. Configuration

Two configuration activities, the Journal Case Category Code and Case Subcategory Code, enable customers to categorize their different use cases for manual journal uploads. For example, suppose you have two other policies for two instances of accruals. In that case, you will create one category code and two subcategory codes inside that these policies can be uploaded into the system and later used by journal cases. 

2.1 Journal Upload Case Category Codes

It is accessible in the following IMG tree: Financial Accounting > Financial Accounting Global Settings > Document > Intelligent Journal Uploads > Define Journal Upload Case Categories

 

2.2 Journal Upload Case Subcategory Codes

It is accessible in the following IMG tree: Financial Accounting > Financial Accounting Global Settings > Document > Intelligent Journal Uploads > Define Journal Upload Case Subcategories

 

3. Posting Policies

You can upload your posting policies in the system with the above settings. In 2023 FPS1, we are only supporting Excel format. To get a template for a posting policy, please get in touch with SAP refer to note: 3561821

A posting policy is a list of instructions for various fields on a standard journal entry header and item levels. A sample file screenshot is as follows:

The Fiori app below creates a posting policy in the system and uploads the document.

Fiori app: Manual Journal Posting Policies (App ID: F8539)

Business Role to access the app: SAP_BR_GL_ACCOUNTANT

The app would be accessible via General Ledger > Journal Entries

Select appropriate category and subcategory codes, describe your policy, and provide a “Valid From” date. Once the policy is active, it will be valid per the provided “Valid From” date. The “Valid To” date is defaulted to 31.12.9999. 

The system validates if the provided “Valid From” date is possible based on the existing active policies for that category and subcategory codes. The system will give an error if an active policy with a “Valid From” date after the provided date exists. The system will allow a “Valid From” date if no active policy with “Valid From” exists on or after that date.

Let’s understand from the illustration below.

If you try to create a new policy with the “Valid From” date 01.07.2024, the system will not allow it as another policy is valid from 15.07. However, the system will allow you to create a new policy valid from 16.07.2024.

In 2023 FPS3, the system cannot detect gaps in datelines for a policy specific to a category and subcategory code. Thus, as the “Valid From” date is a user-entry field, there might be gaps if the user does not ensure proper dates to avoid gaps.

After uploading the policy document, you can proceed with activation. The button Active in the screenshot below activates the document.

Activation is a background process that may take around 4-5 minutes. During activation, all the instructions are extracted from the uploaded policy document and sent to an AI engine to map with the system's existing capabilities. The policy is not activated if the AI engine cannot map one or more instructions. The errors are visible in the Application Log on the Fiori app. In 2023 FPS3, the Fiori app does not have a view to show the mapped capabilities. This is something that is being worked on for the next release cycle.

The supported capabilities are as follows:

  1. SplitConditon: Splitting of journal entries based on specific attributes. For example, journal entries should be split for company codes.
  2. Aggregation: Aggregation of amounts based on specific attributes. For example, the aggregate document currency amount for each cost center and vendor ID.
  3. Copy: Copy the value from an Excel field. For example, the Company ID (BUKRS) value is taken from the Company column in the transaction Excel.
  4. Assign: Assign a fixed value to an attribute. For example, the G/L account has a fixed value defined in the transaction Excel.
  5. Evaluate Condition: Evaluate a condition and then calculate the value for an attribute. For example, Post Key is 40 for positive and 50 for negative amounts.
  6. MaxLines: Set the maximum number of line items in a journal entry. For example, the posting policy may have an instruction stating that a journal entry cannot have more than five line items.
  7. Concatenate: Concatenates different parameters as specified in a posting policy.

Once the policy is active, you can proceed with case creation.

4. Journal Cases

To create a journal case, you should be ready with the data that you want to upload to that case. This data relates to the transactions you prefer to be considered for journal entry creation. There is no standard format for preparing your data except that it should be available as an Excel file.

The first row should be the header row, with the names of the fields corresponding to the data's semantics.

System Validations on data file:

  1. An error will be raised if the column names mentioned in the posting policy file are not present in Excel.
  2. If mandatory fields required to create a journal entry in the system are missing in Excel, an error will be raised.

A sample data file is illustrated below:

 

4.1 Create Journal Case

The Fiori app below creates a journal case in the system and uploads the document.

Fiori app: Manual Journal Upload Cases (App ID: F8538)

Business Role to access the app: SAP_BR_GL_ACCOUNTANT

The app would be accessible via General Ledger > Journal Entries

Describe your case, case category, subcategory codes, and your fiscal period with year and variants while you create a new case. A relevant policy is fetched based on the entered category, subcategory codes, and the last date of the fiscal period. If a policy is found, it is linked under the Guidance section.

You can then upload the Excel file containing transactions. The system will validate the data, either issue an error or accept the file. If the file is accepted, the status changes from Created to Transaction Data Uploaded.

4.2 Generate Proposal

Once the transaction data is uploaded, you can trigger journal entry proposal generation by clicking the Generate Proposal button. Proposal generation is a background process and may take some time. Once the process is over, the details will be available in the Application Log.

If the proposal generation is successful, the status changes to Proposal Generated. If errors occur during the generation process, the status changes to Proposal Generation Failed.

Error handling: If there are errors, you need to go to the Application Log to check them.

  1. If the errors are related to uploaded data, you must fix them in your Excel file. When you have fixed your data in Excel, open the relevant Journal Case and click Edit. Delete the uploaded file from the Source of Transaction Data section, and the status changes to Created. Upload the new file; the status changes to Transaction Data Uploaded. Click the Generate Proposal button again; the status changes to Proposal Generation In Process.

If the errors are related to a system configuration, you must fix them with those settings. After fixing the settings, you can trigger proposal generation again by clicking the Generate Proposal button.

4.3 Validate Proposal

After the successful proposal generation, you can proceed with proposal validation. Click the Validate Proposal button to start the validation process; the status changes to Validation In Process. Validation is a background process and may take some time. Once the process is over, the status is changed accordingly.

In case the validation is successful, the status changes to Proposal Validated. If the validation process runs into errors, the status changes to Validation Failed.

Error handling: If there are errors, you need to go to the Application Log to check them.

  1. If the errors are related to uploaded data, you must fix them in your Excel file. When you have fixed your data in Excel, open the relevant Journal Case and click on the Discard Proposal button. The status changes to Transaction Data Uploaded. Click on Edit and delete the uploaded file from the Source of Transaction Data section, and the status changes to Created. Upload the new file; the status changes to Transaction Data Uploaded. Click the Generate Proposal button again; the status changes to Proposal Generation In Process.
  2. If the errors are related to a system configuration, you must fix them with those settings. After fixing the settings, you can trigger proposal validation again by clicking the Validate Proposal button.

4.4 Posting Proposal

After the successful validation, the user can proceed with the posting by clicking the Post button. This will trigger a background process and may take some time to complete.

On successful postings, the status changes to Complete. Corresponding journal entries (JEs) are created in the relevant general ledgers. The created JE IDs are updated in the proposal line items of the journal case. 

If there is an error, the status changes to Posting Failed. The user can check the errors in the Application Log.

Error handling: If there are errors, you need to go to the Application Log to check them.

  1. If the errors are related to uploaded data, you must fix them in your Excel file. When you have fixed your data in Excel, open the relevant Journal Case and click on the Discard Proposal button. The status changes to Transaction Data Uploaded. Click on Edit and delete the uploaded file from the Source of Transaction Data section, and the status changes to Created. Upload the new file; the status changes to Transaction Data Uploaded. Click the Generate Proposal button again; the status changes to Proposal Generation In Process.

If the errors are related to a system configuration, you must fix them with those settings. After fixing the settings, you can trigger posting again by clicking the Post button.

entry.title: SAP S/4 HANA for Poultry operations covering Breeder, Hatchery, Broiler and Feed Management
entry.content:
Implementing S/4 HANA in Poultry Industry

  1. Grand Parent Management
  2. Breeder Management
  3. Hatchery Management
  4. Broiler Management - Own and Contract Farming
  5. Chicken Processing
  6. Feed Mill Management
  7. Layer Management

Module features are as below along with SAP standards used

Breeder Management

Complete flock calendar generation for vertically linked management of operations from
Breeder, Hatchery and Broiler. Breeder Management covers entire life cycle from shed
placement till liquidation of the batch

Following matrix will detail out standard and customization involved

NoFeatureCustomizationS/4 HANA Transactions
1Flock CalendarFlock Calendar works as MRP for poultry operations across timeline - Screen was customizedComplete Customization
2Batch Schedule and PlacementCustomization for Shed ready activities including mobile AppStandard Procurement and material movement used with hierarchy as Plant and Bin for Batches placed in multiple sheds.
3Flock merging, Shed to shed transferNoStandard Material movement codes used
4Feed, Medicine, IndentValidations for demand addedStandard Inventory request and Material transfer codes used
5Daily TransactionCustomized Screen with mobile AppAccount posting for bird cost
Inventory movements with standard codes including material issue
6Egg CollectionCustomized Screen with mobile AppEgg cost calculation with account posting
Depreciation (Amortization) entries, Material receipt and Material issue for scrap for broken eggs
7Overhead expense Budget and loadingBudget was calculated for per day per bird and per day per egg basis based on flock calendar planningStandard budget and forecast screens were utilized for data inputs for budgets across indirect expense ledgers
8Flock Performance reportsCustomized for poultry requirements with breed standard charts as benchmarkStandard material movement tables and costing tables from CO used for cost

Hatchery Management

Manages 21 days life cycle of hatchery operations from setting to hatching. No calculations and calendars are required for your hatchery manager. Integration with Hatcher and Setter through data acquisition systems for seamless monitoring and alerts.

Hatchery

NoFeatureCustomizationS/4 HANA Transactions
1Setter and Hatcher planningThrough flock calendar linkNone
2Candling TestCustom screen for data entryEWM setup with inventory movements
3Breakout analysisCustom screen for data entryNone
4Hatchery integrationsConfigurationsSCWM/SENSOR (Sensor Data Integration)
5Hatchery performance reportsCustomized reports as per Industry specificsInventory MIS

Broiler Management

Track own farming and contract farming cycle in single solution. Manages multiple locations and farms with roles like lifting supervisor, line supervisor, manager etc. real time bird cost and analytics through reports.

Broiler features

NoFeaturesCustomizationS/4 HANA Transaction
1Shed readyFiori Mobile App for farmNA
2Placement scheduleDOC reservation with hatcheryWM and IM modules transactions
3Batch placementBatch placement screen with mortality and shed selectionIM, MM and WM module transactions
4Daily transactionFeed, Medicine, Vaccine consumption, along with mortality detail. Fiori Mobile App for farmMaterial issue, Material request transactions
5Brid sales and liftingCustomized screen for lifting schedule and weigh scale integrationSales order with catch weight management (CWM) for multiple UoM orders
6Growing charge calculationA/P invoice posting as per GC scheme calculations with incentives and deductions and MSP baseFI transactions with TDS impact
7Chicken weighmentIntegration with bluetooth scales for poultryDelivery / Dispatch transactions

Processing Plant Management

We have utilized standard SAP PP module as base to configure chicken processing operations.

Processing Plant Management helps production managers in planning, measuring and executing the production with least wastage, minimum cost and achieve high utilization of resources for better yield.

NoFeature
1Production forecast based on sales forecast
2Configure multiple BOM with multi level harvesting
4Identify shrinkage at each level of goods movement at multiple level
5Integration with devices for hanging weight to identify exact inputs
6Assign and configure wastage, weight ratio and cost ratios in
7Enable Quality and cost control at each step of process for better quality and quantity of yield.

Feed Mill Management

Feed Mill is and integral part of poultry farming, almost 70% expense of bird cost is driven by feed cost. PoultryOS enables integration with flocks to generate feed production planning with parameter based quality control and approval based material movement.

NoFeature
1Multiple formulas with semi finished and finished good as material for feed production
2Costing for overhead like labor, electricity and material handling
3Resource utilization and capacity utilization planning, track die utilization, machine utilization on daily and production order basis.
4Quality control management with test templates for each item from material inward till finished good consumption
5Production planning with sales and captive consumption forecast
6Production and costing analysis and MIS for tracking and control

With this solution we were able to show critical improvements in business operations and performance and utilization of SAP peaked. With Fiori Apps and BTP integration adoption of solution till last stakeholder like farm line and lifting supervisor was ensured.

Power of SAP put to use by industry experts for poultry not only highlights SAP's strength as a comprehensive operations framework of organizations but also shows its flexibility to adopt.

Sagar Godbole

Team LogicalDNA

SAP S/4HANASAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: SAP Enterprise Support Academy Newsletter February 2025
entry.content:
Dear customers,
Find here an overview of the SAP Enterprise Support Academy monthly newsletter on latest news, training courses, upcoming sessions, and further educational offerings.
The SAP Enterprise Support Academy provides expert guidance and high impact knowledge transfer services that enable you to innovate and be successful with your SAP solutions.

Are you ready to take your ERP and BW transformation to the next level? Check out our series of prerecorded webcasts that guide you through the process of evolving your SAP landscape with SAP Enterprise Support. Learn how to get the most from SAP Enterprise Support Academy and value maps.

Learn more

The Customer Insights dashboard in SAP for Me offers a comprehensive, customizable view of your SAP Solutions. Now, we’ve created a series of short, impactful How-To Trainings videos to help users get the most out of the Customer Insights dashboard.

All details in this blog.

Join our Open Office Hours, every two weeks, on Thursdays, to get answers to your questions around the migration to SAP Cloud Identity Services (AIS/IPS). Next dates: February 13 & 27. Registration is required.

Open Office Hours

Join our new one-hour Meet-the-Expert session to learn capabilities and features of SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity. Learn all about the innovative on-demand solution that connects financial institutions and other financial service providers with their corporate customers on a secure network.

Register here: SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity

Next workshop dates: February 10-13 & March 4-7.

Link to register

Explore co-innovation opportunities with new workstreams, including SAP Analytics Cloud, SAP Datasphere, SAP BTP, Selective Data Transfer to SAP CALM, and the New Service Booking App in SAP4Me. Collaborate, innovate, and gain early access to cutting-edge tools and resources.

Just access Functional Usage/Business Value section in SAP for Me reporting (Customer Insights dashboard).

More details in this blog

This flexible printable report provides you with a detailed case situation insights and a comprehensive analysis of the case volume and trends, resolution time, response levels, case quality and other key performance indicators essential to SAP support cases.

Register for February 6 or 25: SAP Readiness Check for SAP S/4HANA

It covers all major cloud solutions, and it is open to all SAP Enterprise Support customers at no additional cost.
